items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` **Service directory:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/` **Triggered by:** commit 3e09cc59bf9d20ccf2be4279b768de0f7479323a (PR #57631) by `@radhgupta` ## What Changed PR #57631 migrated `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` to a new TypeSpec emitter. This introduced: - A `BackwardCompat/` folder with five new shim files maintaining API compatibility - `metadata` parameter type changed from `IReadOnlyDictionary` to `IDictionary` on client methods across five classes - Multiple enum-like types internalized or removed by the new emitter, now provided as backward-compat stubs - Extension builder class renamed from `AIAgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions` → `AgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions` - 970 API additions and 145 deletions in the public surface ## Gaps Found - **CHANGELOG `1.2.0-beta.11 (Unreleased)` section is completely empty** — the emitter migration and all resulting API surface changes are not recorded anywhere - **Breaking change not documented**: `metadata` parameter type changed from `IReadOnlyDictionary` to `IDictionary` on `CreateAgent`, `UpdateAgent`, `CreateMessage`, `UpdateMessage`, `UpdateRun`, `CreateThread`, `UpdateThread`, `CreateVectorStore`, `ModifyVectorStore` (and their async counterparts) - **Type internalization not documented**: The following types were removed from generated code and are now preserved only as backward-compat stubs — users relying on them directly need to know: `AzureFunctionBindingType`, `FileSearchToolCallContentType`, `MessageDeltaChunkObject`, `PersistentAgentsVectorStoreObject`, `ResponseFormatJsonSchemaTypeType`, `RunStepDeltaChunkObject`, `VectorStoreFileBatchObject`, `VectorStoreFileObject` - **Extension builder rename not documented**: `AIAgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions` renamed to `AgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions` — old name preserved as a stub but callers referencing the new name need guidance - **README missing migration note**: No section or callout explains the emitter migration, backward-compat strategy, or how existing users may be affected when upgrading to `1.2.0-beta.11`
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/CHANGELOG.md` Edit — populate the `1.2.0-beta.11 (Unreleased)` section: ```markdown ## 1.2.0-beta.11 (Unreleased) ### Breaking Changes - The `metadata` parameter type has changed from `IReadOnlyDictionary` to `IDictionary` on the following methods (and their async counterparts): - `PersistentAgentsAdministrationClient.CreateAgent` / `UpdateAgent` - `ThreadMessages.CreateMessage` / `UpdateMessage` - `ThreadRuns.UpdateRun` - `Threads.CreateThread` / `UpdateThread` - `VectorStores.CreateVectorStore` / `ModifyVectorStore` Backward-compatible overloads accepting `IReadOnlyDictionary` are provided and marked `[EditorBrowsable(EditorBrowsableState.Never)]`. Callers passing `Dictionary` or `IReadOnlyDictionary` will continue to compile without changes. - The DI extension class `AIAgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions` (in `Microsoft.Extensions.Azure`) was renamed to `AgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions`. The old name is preserved as a backward-compatible stub. ### Other Changes - Migrated code generation to the new TypeSpec emitter. Several internal enum-like types (`AzureFunctionBindingType`, `FileSearchToolCallContentType`, `MessageDeltaChunkObject`, `PersistentAgentsVectorStoreObject`, `ResponseFormatJsonSchemaTypeType`, `RunStepDeltaChunkObject`, `VectorStoreFileBatchObject`, `VectorStoreFileObject`) that were previously generated are now maintained as backward-compatible stubs to preserve API compatibility. ``` #### File 2: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/README.md` Edit — add a **Migration notice** callout near the top of the `## Key concepts` section (after line 67, before the "### Create and authenticate the client" subsection): ```markdown > [!NOTE] > **1.2.0-beta.11 emitter migration**: This release was regenerated with a new TypeSpec emitter. The `metadata` parameter type changed from `IReadOnlyDictionary` to `IDictionary` on several client methods. Backward-compatible overloads are provided so most callers will compile without changes. If you see overload-resolution warnings or errors after upgrading, switch your `metadata` argument from `IReadOnlyDictionary` to `Dictionary`. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new code snippets are required for these changes — the CHANGELOG and README prose edits above are sufficient. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The existing README contains all required sections in the correct order: 1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the client) 2. ✅ Key concepts 3. ✅ Examples 4. ✅ Troubleshooting 5. ✅ Next steps 6. ✅ Contributing No structural changes are needed; only the migration note addition described in Step 1. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 ai` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 ai` 5. `dotnet format sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [AI Agents Persistent] Docs: Document TypeSpec emitter migration changes in CHANGELOG and README
